# host_systemd_unit (Resource)

Manages one systemd unit file under `/etc/systemd/system`.

This resource writes the unit file and runs `systemctl daemon-reload` after create, update, and delete. Use `host_systemd_service` separately when a `.service` unit should be enabled or running.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_systemd_unit" "backup_service" {
  name = "restic-backup.service"

  content = <<-UNIT
    [Unit]
    Description=Run Restic backup

    [Service]
    Type=oneshot
    ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/restic-backup
  UNIT
}

resource "host_systemd_unit" "backup_timer" {
  name = "restic-backup.timer"

  content = <<-UNIT
    [Unit]
    Description=Run Restic backup every hour

    [Timer]
    OnCalendar=hourly
    Persistent=true

    [Install]
    WantedBy=timers.target
  UNIT
}
```

## Import

Import an existing unit file by name:

```shell
terraform import host_systemd_unit.backup_service restic-backup.service
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `content` (String) Full systemd unit file content.
- `name` (String) Systemd unit file name, such as `example.service` or `backup.timer`.

### Optional

- `mode` (String) Unit file permission mode as four octal digits.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `path` (String) Unit file path under `/etc/systemd/system`.
